intro: Beer-lahai-roi (meaning the well of him that liveth and seeth me or well of the living one who sees me) was the well in the Negev wilderness, between Kadesh and Bered, where the angel of the LORD appeared to Hagar when she fled from Sarai's harsh treatment. There she received the promise of Ishmael's birth and God's provision (Genesis 16:7–14). Isaac later lived near this well and went out to meditate in the field when Rebekah arrived (Genesis 24:62; 25:11). The name commemorates Hagar's remarkable acknowledgment: You are a God of seeing.
hitchcock_meaning: the well of him that liveth and seeth me
